analysis.test: Existence of an established commercial relationship within the meaning of Article L. 442-1 II C. com.: a continuous, stable, and significant relationship […] the parties, characterized by the regularity, volume, and duration of dealings — the element of stability must be the subject of an effective inquiry (Cass. com. [2]).
analysis.faits: Méridian Studios and Cassiopée […] a documented commercial relationship dating back to at least November 2018 (email of 3/11/2018, Exhibit 4), concerning the commercial exploitation of Mistral. Méridian Studios operates under the trade name Bastille (Kbis, Exhibit 1), filed trademark Bastille No. 4449595 (Exhibit 2), placed an order with […] 2 on 07/24/2018 (Exhibit 7), and markets the game on Amazon (Exhibit 6 Bis, screenshot of 01/20/2020). The email of 3/11/2018 explicitly refers to a 'fair' approach in the commercial and marketing relations […] the two parties, evidencing an ongoing collaboration. The judgment of the Tribunal judiciaire Paris of 12/18/2020 (Adverse Exhibit 12) and the ruling of the Cour d'appel Paris of 01/25/2022 (Adverse Exhibit 20) confirm the existence of a relationship […] the parties, even though its subject matter is in dispute.
analysis.faille: Absence of exhibits demonstrating the continuity and regularity of bilateral commercial dealings between Méridian Studios and Cassiopée over a significant period: no recurring inter-party invoices, no successive purchase orders, no framework agreement, no shared revenue records […] the two entities.
analysis.riposte: The existence of a dispute over ownership of the rights does not preclude characterization as an established commercial relationship where the parties have in fact durably collaborated in the commercial exploitation of the game (email of 3/11/2018, orders […] 2, Amazon presence). The case law [2] (Cass. com. 08-21.681) requires an effective search for stability but does not condition the characterization on the absence of any dispute […] between the parties. Furthermore, the tortious nature of liability for abrupt termination (confirmed by [4], Cass. com. 10-11.885) is independent of the validity of the underlying legal title: it is the economic reality of the relationship […] that prevails.

autorites.title: Cass. com. 08-21.681, 05/18/2010
autorites.summary: Ne donne pas de base légale à sa décision la cour d'appel […] qualifie de relation commerciale établie au sens de l'article L. 442-6 I 5° du code de […], la relation […] […] une chaîne de télévision et une société de production indépendante […], pendant huit ans, a fourni des émissions télévisées à la chaîne, sans rechercher si, eu égard à la nature de la prestation de conception et de réalisation de programmes télévisuels, la société de production pouvait légitimement s'attendre à la stabilité de la relation

autorites.title: Cass. com. 10-11.885, 01/18/2011
themes: compétence spéciale en matière délictuelle ou quasi délictuelle
tribunal du lieu où le fait dommageable s'est produit
autorites.summary:
Le fait, pour tout producteur, commerçant, industriel ou personne immatriculée au répertoire des métiers, de rompre brutalement, même partiellement, une relation commerciale établie, sans […] écrit tenant compte de la durée de la relation commerciale et respectant la durée minimale de […] déterminée, en référence aux usages du […], par des accords interprofessionnels, engage, en vertu de l'article L. 442-6 I 5° du code de […], la responsabilité délictuelle de son auteur.
Dès lors, encourt la […] pour violation de ce texte, ensemble l'article 5 § 3 du Règlement (CE) du Conseil n° 44/2001, du 22 décembre 2000, concernant la compétence judiciaire, la reconnaissance et l'exécution des décisions en matière civile et commerciale, un arrêt d'une cour d'appel, statuant sur contredit, […] a retenu qu'une […] d'indemnisation du préjudice résultant la rupture brutale de relations commerciales établies relève de la matière contractuelle au sens de l'article 5 § 1 du Règlement n° 44/2001

analysis.acte_referme: Production of a log of commercial correspondence […] […] Méridian Studios and Cassiopée (emails, letters, meeting minutes […]) covering the period 2018–2021, and/or a certified public accountant's attestation (Camille Lefèvre 3, already referenced in the file) quantifying the financial flows or volumes of business conducted jointly in connection with Bastille.
analysis.moyen_adverse: Cassiopée argues that there is no 'established commercial relationship' within the meaning of L. 442-1 II because Méridian Studios is merely an operator without title, the relationship being in reality a dispute over ownership of the rights in Bastille rather than a stable and reciprocal commercial collaboration; the judicial decisions (TJ Paris 18/12/2020, CA Paris 25/01/2022) would moreover have ruled in Cassiopée's favor on the rights, which […] deprives the purported 'relationship' of any legitimate contractual or commercial basis.
analysis.these_adverse: Cassiopée argues, relying on the judgment of the Tribunal judiciaire Paris of 12/18/2020 (Exhibit 12) and the ruling of the Cour d'appel Paris of 01/25/2022 (Exhibit 20), that the relationship […] the parties never constituted a legitimate and stable commercial collaboration but rather a conflictual relationship arising from Méridian Studios' unauthorized exploitation, which precludes any finding of 'stability' within the meaning of L. 442-1 II. Cassiopée further contends that the email of 3/11/2018 evidences only an isolated exchange and not a continuous and significant relationship, given the absence of documented bilateral financial flows. Finally, the judicial decisions rendered in its favor regarding the rights to Bastille would deprive […] the alleged relationship of any recognized commercial foundation.

pieces_adverses.piece: Jugement TJ Paris 18/12/2020 (Pièce n°12) retenant l'absence de droits de Méridian Studios sur Bastille
pieces_adverses.contre: Ce jugement statue sur la titularité des droits d'auteur, non sur la réalité économique de la relation commerciale ; la rupture brutale est une responsabilité délictuelle autonome indépendante du titre juridique (Cass. com. [4])

pieces_adverses.piece: Arrêt CA Paris 25/01/2022 (Pièce n°20) confirmant le jugement en toutes ses dispositions
pieces_adverses.contre: The appellate decision affirms the ruling on the merits of the rights but does not rule on the existence or duration of an established commercial relationship within the meaning of Article L. 442-1 II; the two […] are distinct

pieces_adverses.piece: Absence of any framework agreement or inter-party invoices produced by Cassiopée to demonstrate the non-existence of the business relationship
pieces_adverses.contre: The absence of a written contract does not preclude the existence of an established commercial relationship; case law [2] recognizes tacit relationships provided that stability is demonstrated by the facts

pieces_attendues: Comprehensive log […] of email/letter exchanges between Méridian Studios and Cassiopée for 2018–2021 (continuity of the relationship)
Expert accountant's attestation from Camille Lefèvre 3 quantifying the financial flows or Bastille volumes involving Cassiopée
Invoices or purchase orders […] between Méridian Studios and Cassiopée during the period of collaboration
Contract or agreement (even informal/by email) defining the terms of the Bastille commercial collaboration […] the two parties
